programmes.show_description: Alf and Mrs Hollingbery are now engaged to be married and he has already started to enjoy her cooking. They treat Arthur, who thinks he's won the pools, to a celebration meal... S4 E3
With Suzi Quatro and Joe Jackson. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
In Rome, Simon cleans up a charity racket in his inimitable style, and paves the way to a new life for a gang of juvenile delinquents. S1 E12: The Charitable Countess
A selection of live performances from TV music shows in the 1960s for a genuine taste of the best of the '60s!

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
By December 1941, did Britain know of the Pearl Harbour plans, and did Churchill warn Roosevelt so he could move the US carriers. Narrated by Robert Powell.

Experts piece together evidence to reveal a giant ring of prehistoric shafts, 20 times the size of Stonehenge and created over 4,000 years ago that has been completely hidden from sight until now. Ep1
Two research teams are granted special permission to investigate what happened to the WWI warship HMS Hampshire and HMS Vanguard, which was commissioned after WWII. Ep10: Scotland's Sea Secrets
...Were You? There are some news events that change the world - in this episode, we take you back to Sunday the 11th of February, 1990 and the release of Nelson Mandela.
From humble beginnings, Barack Obama rose to become the most powerful man in the world - we take a look at his life.
The Yakovlev Yak-1 was a Soviet fighter aircraft of World War II - it was a single-seat monoplane with a composite structure and wooden wings and was a fast and competitive fighter aircraft. S3 E4
The war reaches a critical turning point as Hitler expands it to a second front where German soldiers endure the unrelenting climate of Russia. Ep9: Eastern Front
Our exploration of the elite teams that have led the USA's battle against terror continues with a look at the US Special Forces and the founding of the legendary Green Berets and Navy SEALs.
Examining the history of war tactics. Strategic bombing takes war to the heartland of the enemy. It seeks to destroy not only an enemy's ability to wage war, but also its will to fight on. S2 E3
The series begins before the First World War when military commanders struggled to understand what uses aviation might have. Ep1: Useless For The Purposes Of War
The story of Nazi heavy cruisers and battleships during WWII including the Bismarck, how she got into the Atlantic, and the steps taken to defeat her. Ep5: The Men Who Sank The Bismarck
The machine gun did not dominate the battlefield of WWII - unlike WWI - but it remained a highly potent weapon including being incorporated into the wings of fighter planes. Ep9: The Machine Gun
The 'Desert Fox' Field Marshal Rommel seemed to have a sixth sense when it came to Allied plans and positions - we take a look into how Rommel's winning streak was broken. S1 E10: Rommel's Enigma
The Royal Navy fought a long campaign against the German U-boats and surface ships in the Atlantic; its Pacific Fleet helped win victory over Japan. Ep10: The Royal Navy
Thugs beat Jack up; he knows which 'posh fellas' sent them and plans his revenge as Jessie's husband has a heart-to-heart with Jack and clears the air. S3 E2: A Tiger, A Lamb & A Basket... of Fruit
Arthur's venture into corporate hospitality takes him and Ray into company that is both glamorous and dangerous. S10 E6: All Quiet on the West End Front
While George makes political manoeuvres at home, Ross sails to France to rescue Dwight from the French Revolution. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E5
Drama series following the exploits of Life on Mars's DCI Gene Hunt. Alex smells a conspiracy when the body of a worker at a nuclear research centre disappears from the morgue. S1 E4
Alf, Mrs Hollingbery and Arthur are preparing for their trip to Australia. S4 E4
With The Police. The legendary BAFTA-winning comedy extravaganza from Thames TV (1980). All in the best possible taste!
Simon investigates when an inventor's satellite guidance system falls into the wrong hands and his wife and daughter are used in an attempt to coerce him to defect. S2 E13: The Sporting Chance

Your favourite music from the 1960s - non-stop.
Rudolf Hess flew to Scotland on a secret mission. Decades later he died in prison. Was it suicide or murder. Narrated by Robert Powell.
Part 2 of the documentary examining a team of archeologists who reveal a giant ring of prehistoric shafts just a few miles away from Stonehenge and the ancient henge monument of Durrington Walls. Ep2
...You? There are some news events that change the world - in this episode, we take you back to April 1986 and the Chernobyl disaster and its wider implications.
A look into the lives of the rich and famous; Beyonce rose to fame with Destiny's Child and then went on to forge a solo career as a singer and film star.
London is a huge city with a massive story to tell; we explore the London Underground, the world's first underground transport system with a logo that is recognised by people all over the world.
The F-4 Phantom II is an American tandem two-seat, twin-engine, long-range supersonic jet interceptor and fighter-bomber originally developed by McDonnell Aircraft for the United States Navy. S3 E5

The Gallipoli campaign was the land-based part of a strategy intended to allow Allied ships to pass through the Dardanelles, capture Constantinople, and ultimately knock Ottoman Turkey out of the war.
The story of the price many soldiers had to pay in order for the Allies to take France back from the Germans. Ep10: D-Day and Liberation
We explore some of the most infamous battles in America's fight against terror as the Vietnam war begins, we look at the activities and stories of Medal of Honor recipients and the end of the war.
Examining the history of war tactics. Flank attack - going around the side of the enemy - is a basic manoeuvre, yet stories of victories decided by flanking fill military textbooks. S2 E4
The 1920s and 1930s were uncertain times for the RAF and all other armed services; with peace came cuts in spending on new aircraft development. Ep2: Under Distant Skies
The story of the Blitz of Britain and how Nazi bombers guided to targets by electronic beams were defeated by Dr Reginald Jones in the Battle of the Beams. Ep6: The Man Who Saved Britain's Cities
After WWII, the incredible and innovative weapon systems created by the Third Reich - from jet engines to guided missiles - were uncovered. S1 E11: Secret Weapons of the Third Reich
This programme takes a look at the illustrious history of the Chindits, a group formed by Major General Orde Wingate to fight the Japanese in Burma. Ep11: The Chindits
Arthur announces a house move to Kent to Jessie and says she must accompany him. Sir Horatio refuses to pay Jack for taking part in his housing estate scheme. S3 E3: My Bonnie Lass, Goodbye
Arthur spots a way to make money out of his brother-in-law Brian's misery.
George pursues a vacant seat in Parliament as Agatha plans for her hundredth birthday, and Ross is given a proposition. Starring Aidan Turner. S3 E7
Drama series. Simon Neary is a gangster that Gene has wanted to nail for years, but is stifling his gun deal a way to keep Alex from getting shot in 2008? S1 E5
